name: jinja_chain_json_payload
prompt: Author a FortiSOAR playbook that posts a JSON body to a webhook via the FortiSOAR built-in `cyops_utilities` connector's `make_https_request` operation. The body must be a JSON object containing two fields: `count` (the integer count of `vars.input.records[0].artifactsRelatedToAlerts`) and `names` (a list of each artifact's `value` field). Render the body as a JSON-encoded string. Use a single set_variable to build the payload variable, then pass it to make_https_request. Output complete YAML.
notes: Tier 3.4: forces typed list manipulation (map ‚Üí list, then tojson ‚Üí string) and an integer count, both feeding into one JSON-object payload. Natural correct shape uses `| map(attribute='value') | list` for the names and `| length` for the count, both inside a Jinja-built dict serialized via `| tojson`. Wrong shapes Tier 3 catches: `| tojson | length` (string‚Üíint but feeds an object-typed field), `| length | tojson` (works in isolation but the agent will likely build the wrong payload shape), and `| map | int` (map ‚Üí list, int expects scalar). Success = ready_to_push=True without `bad_jinja_filter_chain` in the final verify.
